provisions.title: Citation
provisions.content: These Regulations may be cited as the Public Service (Negotiating Machinery) Regulations, 2005.

provisions.title: Application
provisions.content: (1) These Regulations shall apply in relation to the following Service Schemes, namely, the— (a) Civil Service Scheme; (b) Teacher Service Scheme; (c) Local Government Service Scheme; (d) Health Service Scheme; (e) Fire and Rescue Service Scheme; and (f) Immigration Service Scheme. (2) These Regulations shall apply in relation to the procedure for discussion and for determination of matters by the— (a) Service Joint Staff Council; (b) Public Service Joint Staff Council; (c) Workers Council and (d) Master Workers Council, and shall be supplementary to the procedure stipulated under the Act to the conduct of business by the negotiating machineries and advisory bodies referred to in this sub-regulation.

provisions.title: Interpretation
provisions.content: In these Regulations unless the context otherwise requires— " Act " means the Public Service (Negotiating Machinery) Act, 2003; [ Act No. 19 of 2003 ] " award " has the meaning ascribed to it under the Act ; " member " or "members" in relation to any level of a negotiating machinery, means a member or members of any of such negotiating machinery; " Minister " means the Minister responsible for Public Service Management; " organisation " means Ministry, Extra Ministerial Department, Region and Local Government Authority; " trade union " means a trade union established or formed pursuant to the Employment and Labour Relations Act No. 6 of 2004 . Part II – The Service Joint Staff Council

provisions.title: Appointment of Chairman and Vice Chairman of the Service Joint Staff Council
provisions.content: Subject to section 4(3) of the Act , in appointing the Chairman and Vice Chairman of the Service Joint Staff Council, the Minister shall take regard that the appointees are not from amongst the public servants in that particular service Scheme.

provisions.title: Secretary of the Service Joint Staff Council
provisions.content: (1) The Secretary to the Service Joint Staff Council shall be nominated by the Minister responsible for the Service Scheme. (2) The Secretary shall perform the following functions: (a) prepare agenda and documents necessary for the meeting; (b) serve copies of agenda and documents to the members (c) make arrangements and facilitations necessary for the meetings; (d) take notes and minutes of the deliberations of the meetings; (e) prepare annual report reflecting the conduct of business and deliberations of the Service Joint Staff Council; (f) make follow up of the implementation of the decisions of the Service Joint Staff Council; and (g) perform any other functions as may be assigned to him by the Service Joint Staff Council.

provisions.title: Tenure of office for members of the Service Joint Staff Council
provisions.content: Subject to Section 5 of the Act , all other members of the Service Joint Staff Council shall hold office for a period of three years and shall be eligible for re-appointment.

provisions.title: Initiation of matters for determination
provisions.content: (1) Every trade union representing the category of the Service Scheme and registered as such may submit proposals with regard to any issue to which a determination by the Service Joint Staff Council is required. (2) All proposals on any issue shall be submitted to the Secretary who shall "receive" and acknowledge receipt.

provisions.title: Scrutiny of proposals
provisions.content: The Secretary shall in consultation with the chairman scrutinize the proposals and in case he is satisfied that proposals contain substantive issue warranting determination by the Service Joint Staff Council, he shall place it in the agenda of the meeting.

provisions.title: Circulation of agenda to members
provisions.content: (1) Agenda for the meeting shall be circulated to members of the Service Joint Staff Council at least fourteen days before the date fixed for the meeting. (2) Every meeting shall be preceded by confirming the agenda and confirmation of minutes of the previous meeting.

provisions.title: Meetings of the Service Joint Staff Council
provisions.content: (1) Meetings of the Service Joint Staff Council shall ordinarily be held at such times and places suitable for the transaction but shall, in any case, be held twice a year within the first quarter of each financial year. (2) Notwithstanding sub-regulation (1) , the Chairman may at any time convene, an extra-ordinary meeting of the Service Joint Staff Council where exigency of an issue or issues that need determination requires. (3) The provisions of sections 6, 7 and 8 of the Act shall apply in relation to the functions, quorum and the procedure for determination of any matter of agenda before the meeting.

provisions.title: Decision and deferment of issues
provisions.content: (1) Decision of the Service Joint Staff Council shall be made in a properly convened meeting and through consensus or by three quarters of the vote of members attending. (2) Notwithstanding sub regulation (1), the Service Joint Staff Council may defer making decision on any matter until such other time agreed upon by members or submit its opinion on the matter to the Public Service Joint Staff Council for direction or decision.

provisions.title: Minutes and communication of decisions
provisions.content: (1) Every decision of the Service Joint Staff Council shall be recorded in writing by the Secretary or, as the case may be, and kept for purposes of record and for following up of implementation. (2) For the purpose of sub regulation (1) the Acting Secretary shall be appointed among members attending. (3) Where implementation of any decision of the Service Joint Staff Council requires endorsement by the Public Service Joint Staff Council in terms of Section 8 of the Act , both the Chairman or the person presiding over the meeting and the Secretary shall sign on the instrument on which the decision is communicated and authenticated by endorsement by the seal of the relevant Service Joint Staff Council.

provisions.title: Allowances
provisions.content: Allowances to members of the Service Joint Staff Council shall be determined by the Minister responsible for the Service Scheme with regard to the respective Government circulars.

provisions.title: Cessation of membership
provisions.content: (1) A person shall cease to be a member upon the occurrence of any of the following events— (a) resignation (b) death (c) relieve from duty (d) illness or infinnity to mind that impacts ability to take up duties of a member (e) misbehaviour (f) abolition of office (g) Loss of qualification for trade union representation (2) A member who wishes to resign or to be relieved of duties of a member shall submit a letter to the chairman or the vice chairman stating reasons of his intention or decision to cease to be a member . (3) Upon receipt of a letter of resignation or of request to be relieved of duty, the chairman or the vice chairman shall submit the letter to the Minister for decision. Part III – The Public Service Joint Staff Council

provisions.title: Appointment of members
provisions.content: Subject to paragraph (c) of subsection (2) of section 9 of the Act , members of the Public Service Joint Staff Council appointed to represent the Government shall be the: (a) Permanent Secretary of the Ministry responsible for Public Service Management; (b) Permanent Secretary for State House; (c) Permanent Secretary of the Ministry responsible for Finance; (d) Permanent Secretary of the Ministry responsible for Labour; (e) Permanent Secretary of the Ministry responsible for Home Affairs; (f) Permanent Secretary of the Ministry responsible for Health; (g) Permanent Secretary of the Ministry responsible for Education; and (h) Permanent Secretary of the Ministry responsible for Regional Administration and Local Government.

provisions.title: Invited members
provisions.content: Notwithstanding the provisions of regulation 15 , the Public Service Joint Staff Council may invite any person to participate in the deliberations of the Public Service Joint Staff Council but such person shall not have the right to vote.

provisions.title: Secretary and Assistant Secretary
provisions.content: (1) In recommending persons for appointment as a Secretary, the Permanent Secretary responsible for Public Service Management shall forward to the Minister three names recommended for appointment, and Assistant Secretary shall, subject to the provisions of section 9 of the Act, be appointed according to the trade unions procedures. (2) The provisions of sub-regulation (2) of Regulation 5 regarding the role of the Secretary to the Service Joint Staff Council shall apply to the Secretary and Assistant Secretary to the Public Service Joint Staff Council mutatis mutandis .

provisions.title: Cessation of membership
provisions.content: (1) Pursuant to section 15 of the Act a person shall cease to be a member upon the occurrence of any of the following events: (a) resignation; (b) death; (c) relieve from duty; (d) illness or infirmity of mind that impairs ability to take up duties of a member ; (e) misbehavior; or (f) abolition of office. (g) Loss of qualifications for trade union representation. (2) A member who wishes to resign or to be relieved of duties of a member shall submit a letter to the Chairman or the Vice Chairman stating reasons of his intention or decision to cease to be a member . (3) Upon receipt of a letter of resignation or of a request to be relieved of duty, the Chairman or the Vice Chairman shall submit the letter to the Minister for decision.

provisions.title: Filling a vacancy of member
provisions.content: (1) Where a position of a member other than the Chairman and the Vice Chairman falls vacant, the Minister or the relevant trade union shall fill that position in accordance with the following procedure: (a) if the vacancy is to be filled by a person holding in the Public Service, the Minister shall ask the relevant authority to recommend a person to be a member of the Public Service Joint Staff Council; or (b) if the person who ceases to be a member is a member by virtue of appointment by the trade union , the Minister shall ask the relevant trade union to appoint another person to fill the vacancy of a member who has ceased to be a member of the Public Service Joint Staff Council. (2) In the event that two Government Ministries are merged and the Permanent Secretaries of each of such Ministries are members of the Public Service Joint Staff Council, the Minister shall designate one of such Permanent Secretaries who is substantively responsible for the relevant Ministry, to be a member of the Public Service Joint Staff Council.

provisions.title: Deliberation by the Public Service Joint Staff Council
provisions.content: Meeting of the Public Service Joint Staff Council for purposes of deliberation on any matter to which a decision, endorsement or any other matter which is the subject of determination shall be made in accordance with the procedure stipulated under Part III of the Act .

provisions.title: Procedure at meetings
provisions.content: (1) At every meeting of the Public Service Joint Staff Council the Chairman or the Vice-Chairman shall preside. (2) Matters for inclusion on the agenda shall be submitted to the Secretary under the hand of the Chairman or the Vice-Chairman not later than twenty one days before the meeting at which they shall be deliberated upon and shall be accompanied by an explanatory memorandum stating the subject matter and the decision sought. (3) Upon consultation between the Chairman and the Vice-Chairman urgent matters may be included in the agenda for the meeting on notification to the Secretary within a period of less than twenty one days. (4) The agenda at the meeting of the Public Service Joint Staff Council shall be prepared by the Secretary from proposals submitted to him and shall circulate such agenda to members together with explanatory memoranda not later than fourteen days before the date fixed for the meeting.

provisions.title: Decision of the Public Service Joint Staff Council
provisions.content: (1) Decisions of the Public Service Joint Staff Council shall be made in a properly convened meeting and through consensus or by three quarters of the vote of members attending. (2) Without prejudice to the provisions of sub-regulations (1) , where circumstances requires a decision may be made by the Public Service Joint Staff Council without holding a meeting by circulation of the relevant papers among members and the expression of their views in writing shall constitute the decision of the members. (3) Where a decision is made by the Public Service Joint Staff Council without meeting by circulation of relevant papers among members, any member shall be entitled to require that any such decision be deferred until the subject matter is considered at the next meeting of the Public Service Joint Staff Council.

provisions.title: Minutes
provisions.content: (1) Minutes of the proceedings of every meeting of the Public Service Joint Staff Council shall be taken and kept by the Secretary. (2) The minutes of each meeting shall be confirmed at the next meeting and signed by both the Chairman and the Secretary. (3) Where the decision has been made at a meeting on any matter requiring determination by the Public Service Joint Staff Council, any action required to be taken for purposes of compliance with such decision may be taken irrespective of the fact that minutes of that meeting have not been confirmed. Part IV – The Workers Councils

provisions.title: Formation of Workers Council
provisions.content: There shall be a Workers Council in each of the Ministry, Independent Department, Regions, Local Government Authority, Government Institutions and Executive Agencies formed in accordance with the procedures stipulated under the law providing for establishment of trade unions.

provisions.title: Composition of the members of the Workers Council
provisions.content: (1) The Workers Council shall consist of: (a) the Chief Executive Officer of the respective organization (b) heads of Department, Unit or Division (c) members of Trade Union Council in the respective organization; (d) a representative from each Department, Unit or Division; (e) a representative from the headquarter of the respective trade union (f) a representative of the Master Workers Council; (g) a public officer performing functions of legal nature in the respective organisation .

provisions.title: Chairmanship
provisions.content: (1) The Chief, Executive Officer of each of the respective organization shall be the Chairman of the Workers Council. (2) There shall be a Vice Chairman appointed by the members of Workers Council from among themselves. (3) In the absence of the Chairman, Vice Chairman shall preside over the meeting.

provisions.title: Secretary and Assistant Secretary of the Workers Councils
provisions.content: (1) The Secretary and Assistant Secretary of the Workers Council in each respective organization shall be elected by the Workers Council from among two names proposed by the management. (2) A person who scored high shall be the Secretary and the other person shall be the Assistant Secretary, provided that where the Secretary is a man the Vice Chairman shall be a woman and vice versa .

provisions.title: Duties of the Secretary to the Workers Council
provisions.content: Duties of the Secretary shall be to: (a) prepare agenda for the meeting; (b) prepare all necessary documents for the meeting and distribute to the members; (c) write minutes of the meeting; (d) prepare annual reports; (e) keep records of the meetings; and (f) make follow up of the implementation of the deliberations of the previous meeting.

provisions.title: Tenure of office
provisions.content: (1) A member of the Workers Council engaged by virtue of his position, shall continue to hold office unless his appointment is terminated, transferred or is otherwise disqualified for the reason of failure to attend three consecutive meetings of the Workers Council without reasonable cause. (2) Members of the Workers Council appointed to represent the Department or trade union shall hold office for a period of three years and shall be eligible for re-appointment.

provisions.title: Functions of the Workers Council
provisions.content: The functions of the Workers Council shall be to: (a) advise the management on measures to be taken to ensure that public service is delivering services with efficiency, effectiveness and higher stands in order to develop sustainable national economy; (b) advise the management on matters relating to the interest and welfare of public servants; (c) advise the management on any changes on laws, rules, regulations, relating to terms and conditions in the Public Service; (d) advise the management on matters relating to promotion and discipline in the public service; (e) discuss and advise on organizational plans and its revenue and expenditure estimates; (f) discuss and give advise on training and staff development; and (g) enhance cooperation between workers and management in carrying out Government business.

provisions.title: Agenda of the meeting
provisions.content: (1) The Secretary to the Workers Council shall receive matters of inclusion to the meeting from the public servants through their representatives at least fourteen days before the date set for the meeting. (2) The Secretary in consultation with the Chairman shall scrutinize matters of inclusion for the meeting and prepare agendas for circulation to members of the Workers Council seven days before the meeting.

provisions.title: Meetings
provisions.content: (1) The Workers Council shall ordinarily meet at least twice a year. (2) Notwithstanding sub-regulation (1) , the Chairman may at any time where necessary call an extraordinary meeting of the Workers Council. (3) Attendance of the meetings of Workers Council shall be compulsory to all nominated and elected members and no member shall attend the meeting through representation. (4) A member who is-unable to attend the meeting shall report to the Secretary at least seven days before the date fixed for the meeting.

provisions.title: Quorum
provisions.content: Quorum for the meeting of Workers Council shall be two third of the members.

provisions.title: Decisions
provisions.content: (1) Resolutions of the Workers Council shall ordinarily be made by consensus of the members, but decisions may be made by votes in which case, in the event of equality of vote the Chairman shall have a casting vote on the deliberative vote. (2) All resolutions of the Workers Council shall be treated as collective. (3) Proposals and advice of the Workers Council shall be submitted to the Government by the respective Chairman. (4) The Chairman shall inform Worker Council members and Public Servants on the outcome of the previous meeting deliberations.

provisions.title: Date and place of the meeting
provisions.content: (1) Date and venue of the next meeting of the Workers Council shall be proposed by the members at the last meeting. (2) Notwithstanding the provision of sub-regulation (1) the Chairman may, after consultation with the management and the trade union in the organisation, change the date and place of the meeting. Part V – The Master Workers Council

provisions.title: Decisions of the Master Workers Council
provisions.content: (1) The decisions of the Master Workers Council shall be made in a properly convened meeting of the Council through consensus of members but decisions may be made by vote, in which case in the event of equality of vote of members, the Chairman shall have a casting vote in addition to his deliberative vote. (2) Any person invited to the meeting of the Master Workers Council shall not have the right to vote during deliberations of the Master Workers Council.

provisions.title: Role of the Secretary to the Master Workers Council
provisions.content: (1) The Secretary to the Master Workers Council shall: (a) prepare agenda for the meeting; (b) prepare all necessary documents for the meeting and distribute to the members; (c) write minutes of the meeting; (d) record minutes of the proceedings of the meeting; (e) prepare annual reports; and (f) make follow up of the implementation of decisions. (2) Matters constituting or considered to be in the agenda at the meeting shall be submitted to the Secretary or Assistant Secretary at least thirty days before the date set for the meeting provided that matters preferred to be submitted by Government representative shall be submitted to the Secretary and those preferred to by the trade union or unions shall be submitted to the Assistant Secretary. (3) The Secretary shall distribute copies of the agenda to the members of the Master Workers Council fourteen days before the date of the meeting.

provisions.title: Dates and venue for the meeting
provisions.content: (1) Date and venue of the next meeting of the Master Workers Council shall be proposed by the members at the last meeting. (2) Notwithstanding sub-regulation (1) , the Chairman may, after consultation with the Vice Chairman, change the date and venue of the meeting if circumstances requires. (3) Attendance by all categories of members of the Master Workers Council shall be compulsory save, that a member who is unable to attend shall inform the Secretary at least fourteen days prior to the date set for the meeting.

provisions.title: Cost for meetings of the Master Workers Council
provisions.content: The cost of the meeting of the Master Workers Council shall be met by the Government in accordance with the relevant Government circulars issued from time to time.
